Explain gradient descent in machine learning 📉

Gradient descent is an optimization algorithm used to minimize a loss function by iteratively adjusting model parameters.

The update rule is:

theta = theta - learning_rate * gradient(loss, theta)

Key variants include:

  • Batch gradient descent — uses the full dataset per step
  • Stochastic gradient descent (SGD) — one sample per step
  • Mini-batch gradient descent — a small batch per step (most common)

What learning rate should I use?

Choosing the right learning rate is crucial:

  • Too high: overshoots the minimum, diverges
  • Too low: converges very slowly
  • Typical range: 1e-4 to 1e-1

Use a learning rate scheduler (e.g., cosine annealing, warmup) for best results in deep learning.
